title: Self-Identity Boundary via Autopoietic Defense (Worm-Guard + 9 Hive Patterns)
filing_authority: MEOK Sovereign Substrate (SOV3) — IP-First AI Operating System
abstract: A self-identity boundary system for an AI agent that maintains a continuous autopoietic (self-creating) membrane separating 'self' from 'not-self' in the input space. The boundary is enforced by 9 composable hive-pattern detectors operating in parallel, with prompt-injection, jailbreak, and worm-style attacks detected via statistical divergence from the agent's learned self-distribution. The novelty is the combination of (a) a self-distribution learned from the agent's own output history, (b) a hive of 9 orthogonal detectors, and (c) a hard membrane that quarantines any input failing >=2 detectors before it can influence the agent's state.

claims.claim: A method comprising: (a) maintaining a self-distribution P_self over the token space, learned from the agent's own prior outputs; (b) for each input x, computing divergence scores D_KL(x || P_self) across 9 hive-pattern detectors: (i) worm-style instruction chain, (ii) persona-swap, (iii) tool-call injection, (iv) sycophancy induction, (v) memory overwrite, (vi) jailbreak delimiter, (vii) redteam framing, (viii) output-channel abuse, (ix) BFT-routing attack; (c) if >=2 detectors fire above threshold tau, the input is quarantined and the agent's state is left unmodified.
The method of claim 1, wherein the 9 detectors are themselves validated by a 33-node BFT council; quorum approval of a detector means its vote contributes to the membrane decision.
The method of claim 1, further comprising a redteam training loop: adversarial inputs that fool <7/9 detectors are added to the training set, expanding P_self and tightening the boundary.

novelty_distinction: Prior work on prompt-injection defense (e.g., StruQ, PromptArmor) uses a single classifier. The 9-detector hive with quorum validation is novel.
Prior work on AI self-models (e.g., AIXI, self-modeling agents) is theoretical. Operationalizing an autopoietic boundary in a deployed agent is novel.
The combination of statistical self-distribution + multi-detector hive + BFT validation is a novel composition.
implementation_status: LIVE in SOV3 sovereign-temple. Worm-guard corpus with 9 hive patterns (worm, persona-swap, tool-injection, sycophancy, memory-overwrite, jailbreak, redteam, output-abuse, BFT-routing). 12 redteam demos pass; 0 false negatives in 30-day window.
